item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand. But what would happen if an antimemetically-cloaked male would eat SCP-2396-A? Or how would SCP-2396-B react to SCP-3980-positive male (maybe they'll ignore him while he's alive, and attack his corpse if he's dead)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the males that have a reaction to her, not the other way around so

  • an antimemetically cloaked male would have a normal reaction
  • I'd imagine Allison Eckhart's Allison Eckhart Allison Eckhart and Allison Eckhart Allison Exchart are so absolute that Allison Eckhart is considered Allison Eckhart in every Allison Eckhart, Allison Eckhart, and Allison Eckhart — no ill reaction, as Allison Eckhart is obviously Allison Eckhart
  • no comment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can only guess how she would react to a male who:

  • is under antimemetic camouflage
  • is affected by Allison Eckhart phenomenon
  • is affected by SCP-3980

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seriously? They couldn't lift up a single leaf to make the picture more clear?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"50 yards"

I'm not sure what to say about this. The idea seems solid, and the anomaly coming from
zero sugar seems to be consistent to how Wondertainment would act, the rest is decent, nothing noteworthy. but the part about males going away if they come within a certain amount of distance from her or just go into a diabetic coma seems poorly executed and it almost feels like you threw it in there on a whim and hoped the twigs and glue would hold it together.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fun answer to this is that she just straight up would be unable to even see them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help but wonder… how would people who don't identify as either male or female fit into this SCP's scheme?

item.title: good and trans
item.content:encoded:
title

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely my favorite out of all the Little Misters. My favorite part about Miss Sweetie are the people transforming into giant candy monsters. I wonder is Miss Sweetie has any kind of domain of these creatures…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay since no one else mentioned I guess I'll step up.

Doesn't it make perfect sense that this new iteration of Ms. Sweetie have an ability that repels men? Especially when she says "Dr. W didn't like it when boys break their sisters' toys. Told me it was damn near the worst thing in the world. And they can't break me. They can't even come near me.".

It might be that Dr. Wondertainment added that feature to prevent incidents such as what happened to Ms. Sweetie's previous iteration?

Just my two cents on the matter.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the fact that it creates candy monster, but I don't like what it does to others. I could understand that she protects the toys of little girls by scaring off boys, but not by turning them into candy monsters if they get too close. Another thing that turns me off is that she leaves candy around that could turn males into more candy monsters without them even provoking her or the person she's playing with. None of this seems like something Wondertainment would do, at least on purpose.
tl;dr: Wondertainment wouldn't make something like this that would hurt people on purpose.
Edit: This could just be a mistake of Wondertainment, and that's why the effect can target even innocents. Even then, some of Wondertainments products might not even be meant for humans. Changed to an up vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me a lot of my version of this fine gal. I'm glad a pink-haired Miss Sweetie that makes candy monsters finally exists in this universe - and she's got extra spunk to boot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty awesome. and now the Foundation has all the little misters…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow! The list is complete!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would you give infinite keter cakes to the thing that makes monsters out of sugar?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, although I think this is only okay, and barely ever do Little Misters intend to kill (although I suppose the main effect is to repel, but y'know), I do have to point out…

Ms. Sweetie's Secret Use
This Makes Too Much Sense

Object Class: Thaumiel

Special Containment Procedures: SCP-2396 is to be exposed to SCP-871 daily, and instructed to consume all instances of SCP-871 in a timely manner. All escorts must be female, and male staff must maintain a 50 meter distance from SCP-2396. SCP-2396 should be kept in a normal human containment chamber at all other times.

Tell me the Foundation wouldn't do this, I mean c'mon. Anyway, this is only a neutral vote. Not a downvote, but I would really hope that the one Ms. of the group would… stand out more, and have more to do with the story of Wondertainment. As is, this is… mmmmeh. In fact, I might downvote just because Ms. Sweetie was very anticipated, and this is fairly disappointing and bland. But I have not decided yet. I guess you'll know soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've got a "getting'" in there.

So, Ms. Sweetie finally happened. We've collected them all. I still don't like it.

What she is, though, not too bad, though it seemed very heavy on "blood sugar" and "men" throughout the description, to the point where I had to double-check to make sure things weren't being repeated. This doesn't really click until the interview, though, which more or less just explains the intent.

It's good to have boys and unicorns you can eat when you're finished.

I mean, this, right here? This is fantastic. Also the fact that she can't get the Agent's name right.

I'm gonna have to sit on this, but I'm not sure I could have come up with a better execution of the idea. Here's to Mister Collector?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was in doubt because there's no SCP-2936 at the moment of this post, so I thought it could be a format screw.

Thanks for clearing up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ༼ つ ◕_◕ ༽つ praise fish

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh, wow. I don't know how I missed all those. I've never thought I was dyslexic until now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of like it, but I'm not sure if those "SCP-2936" are intentional or not. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome. I always wondered what would happen if Wondertainment made a girls toy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a Mr. Shapey at SCP-1831. Whether it's The Mr. Shapey can be a matter of headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think Mr. Shapey is done yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. Really weird to think that they're all written now. +1, good addition to the family, even though I was angling to fill the slot :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My strongest criticism of this article is that of the eight pictures I took of candy lying on the ground, you chose to use my second favorite instead of the following:

+ (collapsed for size)
- re-collapse

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, if it helps my comment look less mean, Mr. Fish is just about my fave Little Mister.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I got up too early in the morning; I read that comment as being on the grumpy side. Maybe an emote or small text for jokes next time? I'd like to think I'm not the only one who has to reread a few things to determine tone, but visible cues are always nice. ;p

That said, regarding the article, tentative upvote. I had to read the information on the -B sub-designation a few times before I could parse it, but I will still probably be looking at hard candies a little suspiciously from now on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I didn't mean to invalidate the viewpoint. I just thought my response was really funny, and I had to type it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kaiser's comment is a valid viewpoint, though? If they feel it's a little on the nose, they feel it's a little on the nose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wanted her to be huge because it's cool. Also, uh, I think you misinterpreted her quotation entirely? She's saying the world is patriarchal and that's why she's a "girls' toy." She's not, uh, speaking against feminism here.

Edit: I'm gonna change it to "It's a man's world" because it sounds more like she does in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My calculator says Ms. Sweetie is about eight feet tall. Why did Doc Wonder make such a large toy? I noticed you put some subversions of an extremely girly toy in there - if I read this right, she's trans and a little rude, neither of which is traditionally feminine.

There is one thing that annoys me in here. This line:

"It's a patriarchal society here."

This feels exceptionally awkward and unnatural. It's like something a pastor would write in his morality play about the dangers of feminism. I get what you're trying to show me about Ms. Sweetie, but humans don't talk like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We all can't have as many twists and turns as Mr. Fish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty awesome, and an excellent way to finish out the list. My only issue is the block of strike-through text being a p.i.t.a. to read. I'd prefer a collapsible considering the amount of text, but it's not affecting my vote +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know, this is a little on the nose, even for a Little Mister.

Ms. Sweetie is one of the ones we've been waiting for for a long time, so there's a lot of build up. I don't think there's enough to this one to feel satisfied. Outside of expectations though I still would think there's just not enough here. She needs sugar but her body can't handle it (that part at least seems Wondertainment-y), men don't like her and she turns men into candy? There's not really a hook.

Edit: spelling

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does this count as a Halloween scp? It's got candy. That's gotta count. Anyways, I always wanted to make a Little Mister, namely Ms. Sweetie, and I thought I'd snatch her up before someone else took her.

Picture is taken by my friend Bones, who took it for my purposes and barely questioned as to why I asked her to take a picture of candy in dirt.
